content.problemStatement:
This problem has subtasks.

Alice and Bob are playing a game with a binary string SSS of length NNN. The game involves two steps:


Alice's Move: Alice can remove any single substring from SSS. The remaining parts of the string stay in the same order.
Bob's Move: After Alice's move, Bob can select any substring of the modified string and flip all its characters. Flipping SiS_iSi​ means it becomes 111 if it was originally 000, and vice versa.The game ends after that.
If both players make their moves optimally, with Alice aiming to maximize the lexicographical value of the final string and Bob aiming to minimize it, what will be the resulting string?

Note:



The empty subsequence is lexicographically the smallest of all.




A substring of a string is obtained by deleting some (possibly zero) characters from the beginning and some (possibly zero) characters from the end. Thus, an **empty string is also a valid substring** of SSS.




String XXX is lexicographically smaller than string YYY, if either of the following satisfies:



XXX is a prefix of YYY and X≠YX \neq YX=Y.
There exists an index iii such that Xi<YiX_i \lt Y_iXi​<Yi​ and Xj=Yj,∀jX_j = Y_j, \forall jXj​=Yj​,∀j such that 1≤j<i1 \leq j \lt i1≤j<i.



Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN — the length of SSS.
The next line contains the binary string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the final string SSS.
constraints: SSS is a binary string, i.e, contains only the characters 000 and 111.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.
**Subtask 111 (202020 points):** The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 500050005000.

Explanation:
Test case 111:

Alice's move: [0]→Do Nothing or Choose Empty Substring [0][0] \xrightarrow{\text{Do Nothing or Choose Empty Substring }} [0][0]Do Nothing or Choose Empty Substring ​[0]
Bob's move:  [0]→Do Nothing or Choose Empty Substring [0][0] \xrightarrow{\text{Do Nothing or Choose Empty Substring }} [0][0]Do Nothing or Choose Empty Substring ​[0]

Test case 222:

Alice's move: [01]→Do Nothing or Choose Empty Substring [01][01] \xrightarrow{\text{Do Nothing or Choose Empty Substring }} [01][01]Do Nothing or Choose Empty Substring ​[01]
Bob's move:  [01]→Flip Substring from l=1 to r=1[00][01] \xrightarrow{\text{Flip Substring from } l=1 \text{ to } r=1} [00][01]Flip Substring from l=1 to r=1​[00]

Test case 333: There can be many different ways; one such way is:

Alice's move: [1101]→Delete Substring from l=0 to r=0[101][1101] \xrightarrow{\text{Delete Substring from }l=0 \text{ to } r=0} [101][1101]Delete Substring from l=0 to r=0​[101]
Bob's move:  [101]→Flip Substring from l=0 to r=0[001][101] \xrightarrow{\text{Flip Substring from } l=0 \text{ to } r=0} [001][101]Flip Substring from l=0 to r=0​[001]
